The font size looks too small, make it bigger

<think>
I see the note editor. The text appears small. I need to either:
1. Use Cmd+= (Cmd+Plus) to zoom in
2. Go to Settings and adjust the Font Size slider
3. Use the Format menu

Cmd+= is the fastest approach.
</think>

{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "=", "modifiers": ["cmd"]}

I accidentally deleted my note, can you undo it?

<think>
The user deleted a note and wants to undo. In Epistemos, Cmd+Z should work if the deletion was recent. The undo stack in NSTextView handles this through shouldChangeText/didChangeText.
</think>

{"action": "key", "selector": "", "value": "z", "modifiers": ["cmd"]}

Show me what's in my vault

<think>
The user wants to see their vault contents. I can either:
1. Use the list_files tool to show vault contents
2. Navigate to the notes sidebar which shows all files

The notes sidebar is the visual approach. Let me also check the file list.
</think>

{"action": "click", "selector": "AXOutline[@AXTitle='Sidebar']"}

I want to work in fullscreen mode

<think>
The user wants fullscreen. On macOS, I can use the green traffic light button, or Ctrl+Cmd+F, or Fn+F. The keyboard shortcut is most reliable.
</think>

Split my screen between Epistemos and Safari

<think>
Split screen on macOS: hover over the green window button → choose tile left/right. Or use keyboard: Ctrl+Option+Left/Right. The keyboard approach is faster.
</think>
